project: Maple Street Medical Office Building (3 storey)

line_items.description: 5/8" Type X gypsum board, both faces

exclusions.quote: this proposal EXCLUDES fire-stopping/firecaulking at rated assemblies
exclusions.risk: Fire-stopping is a life-safety requirement for rated assemblies. This cost will need to be covered by another trade or a separate contract.
Any work requiring scaffolding above 10 feet will incur additional costs for rental or provision by another party.
The general contractor or another trade will be responsible for final cleaning of the work area.
The general contractor or another trade is responsible for removing and hauling off construction debris generated by this scope of work.

assumptions.text: Pricing assumes building dried-in and conditioned prior to board installation.
One mobilization included; additional mobilizations billed at $850 each.
Pricing valid for 30 days from date above. Material escalation may apply thereafter.
Work is based on Architectural drawings dated 05/2026.

alternates.text: Add alternate ADD-1: Level 5 finish at lobby walls

scope_gaps.why: The quote includes 'Ceiling suspension grid framing' but does not mention the actual acoustic ceiling tiles or panels that would typically be installed within that grid.
Drywall installation often requires wood blocking or backing for mounting fixtures, grab bars, or reinforcing walls, which is not explicitly mentioned.
Access panels for mechanical, electrical, or plumbing systems within drywall assemblies are a common requirement but are not listed in the scope.
Standard practice for drywall contractors includes protecting adjacent finished surfaces during their work, but this is not explicitly stated.
If the project involves phased work or occupied spaces, temporary partitions and dust control measures are often required but not mentioned.

notes: High confidence in extraction; document is clear and well-structured.
